SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The algorithm is presented with clear pseudocode (Algorithm 1) and is grounded in prior bandit/halving work (SUCCESSIVEHALVING, Jamieson & Talwalkar 2015). Empirical evaluation is extensive (multiple datasets, repeated trials, comparisons to SMAC/TPE/Spearmint/random and variants) and demonstrates large speedups in several settings. The paper defers a full theoretical treatment and formal proofs to Li et al. (2016), so the stronger theoretical claims (log-factor near-optimality) are not proven within this manuscript. Some experimental choices (e.g., inability to run Fabolas, limited convergence runs on expensive NN tasks, and lack of reported statistical tests) are minor concerns but do not invalidate the main technical claims.

ORIGINALITY.justification: HYPERBAND combines existing ideas (random search, SUCCESSIVEHALVING/halving-style bandit algorithms) in a novel and practical way by hedging over different choices of n via multiple brackets to address the n vs B/n tradeoff. The formulation is a clear, non-trivial extension of prior halving approaches and emphasizes a generally applicable resource allocation perspective (iterations, data, features). While it builds on well-studied techniques, the specific bracketed strategy and systematic empirical validation constitute a notable and useful contribution rather than a purely incremental rehash.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well-structured (motivation, related work, algorithm with pseudocode, examples, experiments, appendix) and explains key inputs and parameters (R, η, brackets) clearly, with an illustrative LeNet example. Figures and tables are referenced to support intuition and results, and required subroutines are specified (get_hyperparameter_configuration, run_then_return_val_loss, top_k). The manuscript sometimes compresses or defers formal theory (pointed to Li et al. (2016)) and the provided text contains some repeated passages and minor editorial issues, but overall the method and experiments are communicated clearly.
